comments.raw_text: This is still reproducible, I filled bug 2013522 as a follow-up.

bug_analysis.root_cause: Unified search button was not displayed on about:newtab page in private browsing windows due to about:newtab and about:privatebrowsing being handled differently, treated as non-blank pages.
symptoms: Unified search button missing when navigating to about:newtab on private browsing window
Unified search button present when navigating to about:home on private browsing window
reproduction_steps: Open a private browsing window in Firefox
Observe that the unified search button is missing
Navigate to about:home in the private window
Observe that the unified search button is present
bug_analysis.technical_details: Patch attempted to handle about:newtab and about:privatebrowsing as blank pages to fix the missing unified search button issue. The initial patch was pushed but later backed out due to causing test failures in browser_primaryUI.js. Multiple crash reports linked with signatures such as 'JSClass::doFinalize', 'mozilla::layers::NativeLayerCA::NextSurface', and 'nsGlobalWindowOuter::PrepareForProcessChange'.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Users in private browsing mode do not see the unified search button on the new tab page, potentially reducing search functionality or discoverability.

common_patterns: Crashes linked to finalization of JavaScript objects
Crashes triggered in private browsing or about:newtab contexts
Unified search button missing coinciding with crash reports
potential_root_causes: Use-after-free or premature finalization of JS objects related to unified search UI components
Improper lifecycle management of JS objects during private window initialization or teardown
Race conditions or dangling pointers in the search button's JS bindings
relevant_fix_clues: Fixes likely involve correcting JS object lifecycle and finalization order
Ensuring that JS objects related to UI elements are retained properly until safe to finalize
Patches may add checks to prevent finalizing objects still in use or referenced
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes in JSClass::doFinalize are linked to improper management of JavaScript object lifecycles, particularly around the unified search button in private windows or new tab pages. The missing unified search button symptom indicates UI elements are prematurely finalized or corrupted, leading to crashes. Fixes typically address the root cause by improving object retention and finalization logic to avoid use-after-free scenarios.

common_patterns: Crashes linked to finalization of JavaScript objects
Crashes triggered in private browsing or about:newtab contexts
Unified search button missing coinciding with crash reports
potential_root_causes: Use-after-free or premature finalization of JS objects related to unified search UI components
Improper lifecycle management of JS objects during private window initialization or teardown
Race conditions or dangling pointers in the search button's JS bindings
relevant_fix_clues: Fixes likely involve correcting JS object lifecycle and finalization order
Ensuring that JS objects related to UI elements are retained properly until safe to finalize
Patches may add checks to prevent finalizing objects still in use or referenced
similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes in JSClass::doFinalize are linked to improper management of JavaScript object lifecycles, particularly around the unified search button in private windows or new tab pages. The missing unified search button symptom indicates UI elements are prematurely finalized or corrupted, leading to crashes. Fixes typically address the root cause by improving object retention and finalization logic to avoid use-after-free scenarios.

refined_analysis.root_cause: In private browsing windows, about:newtab pages are treated as non-blank, preventing initialization of the unified search button. Attempts to mark about:newtab as blank caused premature JS object finalization (JSClass::doFinalize) and use-after-free crashes due to improper lifecycle management of the button's JS objects.
symptoms: Unified search button missing on about:newtab pages in private browsing windows
Unified search button present on about:home pages in private browsing windows
reproduction_steps: Open Firefox in a private browsing window
Navigate to about:newtab and verify the absence of the unified search button
Navigate to about:home and verify the button is visible
refined_analysis.technical_details: A reverted patch that treated about:newtab as a blank page to enable the unified search button caused JSClass::doFinalize crashes, indicating premature finalization of JS objects managing the button. These lifecycle management errors result in use-after-free crashes during private browsing setup or teardown.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Private browsing users lack access to the unified search button on new tab pages, reducing discoverability of search features.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: In browser/modules/AboutNewTab.sys.mjs, audit the unified search button’s JS object lifecycle for private browsing contexts. Specifically, identify and adjust lifecycle methods (e.g., initialization, finalization) to prevent premature JSClass::doFinalize invocation. Implement reference counting or guard checks to retain JS objects until safe finalization, enabling the button on about:newtab without triggering crashes.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: The unified search button is missing on about:newtab pages in private browsing windows because about:newtab is treated as a non-blank page, blocking button initialization in browser/modules/AboutNewTab.sys.mjs. Previous attempts to treat about:newtab as blank caused premature JS object finalization (JSClass::doFinalize) and use-after-free crashes. Fix requires correcting the unified search button’s JS lifecycle management in AboutNewTab.sys.mjs for private browsing.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The current analysis correctly identifies the root cause, symptoms, and affected components but is overly verbose and contains redundant information. It lacks explicit references to specific functions or code paths managing the unified search button lifecycle, reducing precision for an AI-driven fix. The reproduction steps, while clear, can be shortened for conciseness. The proposed fix is high-level and does not break down concrete steps or mention specific lifecycle methods or JS objects involved. The optimized_context is too long and partly redundant, missing a sharp, concise summary that ties directly to the relevant module and lifecycle management. Overall, it is mostly self-contained but would benefit from tighter scope, explicit artifact hints (e.g., lifecycle functions or classes within AboutNewTab.sys.mjs), and a clearer, actionable solution sketch.

filtered_context.concise_summary: The unified search button is missing on about:newtab pages in private browsing because about:newtab is treated as non-blank, blocking button initialization in browser/modules/AboutNewTab.sys.mjs. Attempts to treat about:newtab as blank caused premature JSClass::doFinalize finalization of the button's JS objects, leading to use-after-free crashes. The fix requires correcting the button's JS object lifecycle management in AboutNewTab.sys.mjs to safely enable the button on about:newtab in private windows.
core_symptoms: Unified search button missing on about:newtab in private browsing windows
Button present on about:home in private browsing windows
technical_constraints: Avoid premature JSClass::doFinalize finalization of unified search button JS objects
Ensure proper lifecycle management (initialization/finalization) of button JS objects in private browsing context
Prevent use-after-free crashes caused by premature object finalization
